title: toxicodendron rostratum
intro: Toxicodendron rostratum, also known as the Australian poison tree, is a plant species native to the eastern coast of Australia that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its shiny green leaves and red or yellow berries. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic oil that can cause skin irritation, but it has also been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Toxicodendron rostratum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, while also being used as a natural antiseptic and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, and it is also used in some traditional remedies to treat wounds and promote healing.
